title: I have a huge bunch of Evangelion 1996 cards with Sega on them
display_title: I have a huge bunch of Evangelion 1996 cards with Sega on them
wikitext:
[]: I have a huge bunch of Evangelion 1996 cards with Sega on them             Quote Post by CaptainKBD » Sun Jan 19, 2025 8:35 pm It is proving very difficult to find info on them, but i do have this resource: https://www.arcadegamecards.com/product ... 996-amada/ that lists the cards. i found them in a small suitcase in a Japanese store here in thailand and they are all basically brand new in their paper packets. They have some cool scenes from the series and i have most of the cards. Has anyone seen these before or know ānything else about them?

[]: Re: I have a huge bunch of Evangelion 1996 cards with Sega on them             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Mon Jan 20, 2025 1:08 am Are those like trading cards? Because those are very common for anime of the time. I recognize all the artwork from elsewhere. Still nice of course.

[]: Re: I have a huge bunch of Evangelion 1996 cards with Sega on them             Quote Post by gloomyAmateur » Mon Jan 20, 2025 12:12 pm I've never seen these before. I wonder where they were sold?

[]: Re: I have a huge bunch of Evangelion 1996 cards with Sega on them             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Mon Jan 20, 2025 1:42 pm Sega had a relationship with the show for the entire duration. You'll find Sega branded merch, for instance an MPE plushie included with the EoE deluxe laserdisc set. And of course their logo shows up any time you start EoE, since they were one of the funders. Most importantly, they released the first 2 Eva games: NGE for Saturn and its sequel, 2nd Impression. As well as others; Eva was a Sega-only franchise for a bit, in which all toys and games were exclusive. Those cards were a tie-in with those games. Each game came bundled with 3 additional game-only character/angel cards, and there was a campaign where you could send in proof of purchase of both games to receive a final 3 cards to complete the whole set. You can see pics here at the bottom: https://www.satakore.com/sega-saturn-ga ... n-JPN.html EDIT: Oh, and in fact, Sega released a game on Saturn that was a card library, called exactly that, "NGE Digital Card Library." I believe it contains all the cards to view, and in the game they're accompanied by clips. You unlock things by playing minigames, and there are gag comics.

[]: Re: I have a huge bunch of Evangelion 1996 cards with Sega on them             Quote Post by CaptainKBD » Fri Jan 31, 2025 12:15 am Thanks Axx0N N - the picture list i posted is more accurate. i can take photos of all the cards i have as its nearly teh full collection and htey are brand new, astouding for something nearly 20 years old. some really cool moments in the series are captured.
